content.text:
Can you add this to the readme.md?


Concept that was years in my mind and  discussed with other photographers like Paul Disario and Michael Goldberg for many years, and finally got woken up/initialized by the app from Peter Blakely (FotoArch/MetaEdit) that was Mac based only

result.output:
1	# ImageArc
2	
3	Open-source, cross-platform (Linux/Windows/macOS) AI photo captioning and
4	IPTC/XMP metadata tool for photographers. Pure Go, no CGO, single static binary.
5	
6	ImageArc never rewrites your image pixels. It calls out to
7	[ExifTool](https://exiftool.org) to write standard IPTC/XMP metadata: in-file
8	for JPEG/TIFF, and Lightroom-style `.xmp` sidecars for RAW (CR2/CR3/NEF/ARW/DNG/...).
9	
10	## Features
11	
12	- **AI captioning** via local [Ollama](https://ollama.com) (llava, qwen2.5vl,
13	  qwen3-vl) — fully implemented, works offline.
14	- Provider interface ready for Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, and any
15	  OpenAI-compatible endpoint (stubs included, easy to fill in).
16	- Non-destructive metadata writes through ExifTool: caption, keywords, byline,
17	  location, headline, date — mapped to both IPTC and XMP fields.
18	- Recursive folder pipeline with a configurable worker pool and live progress.
19	- CSV catalog builder, with or without AI (`--no-ai` just reads existing IPTC).
20	- Per-client JSON templates (byline, location, keyword prefixes, caption prefix).
21	- Embedded dark-theme web UI: batch runner with a live activity log (SSE),
22	  metadata editor, and catalog builder — no external CDNs, one binary.
23	- No lock-in: everything lands in plain CSV, JSON, and XMP/IPTC.
24	
25	## Install
26	
27	Requirements:
28	- Go 1.22+ (to build from source)
29	- [ExifTool](https://exiftool.org) on your `PATH`
30	  - Linux: `sudo apt install libimage-exiftool-perl` (or your distro's package)
31	  - macOS: `brew install exiftool`
32	  - Windows: download from exiftool.org and add to `PATH`
33	- [Ollama](https://ollama.com) for local AI captioning, plus a vision model:
34	  ```
35	  ollama pull llava
36	  # or: ollama pull qwen2.5vl / ollama pull qwen3-vl
37	  ```
38	
39	### Build
40	
41	```
42	go build -o imagearc ./cmd/imagearc
43	```
44	
45	### Cross-compile
46	
47	```
48	GOOS=linux   GOARCH=amd64 go build -o dist/imagearc-linux-amd64     ./cmd/imagearc
49	GOOS=windows GOARCH=amd64 go build -o dist/imagearc-windows-amd64.exe ./cmd/imagearc
50	GOOS=darwin  GOARCH=arm64 go build -o dist/imagearc-macos-arm64     ./cmd/imagearc
51	```
52	
53	## Usage
54	
55	### Caption a folder
56	
57	```
58	imagearc caption ./photos --provider ollama --model llava --recurse
59	```
60	
61	Dry run (caption only, no metadata written):
62	
63	```
64	imagearc caption ./photos --dry-run
65	```
66	
67	With a client template and a custom worker count:
68	
69	```
70	imagearc caption ./photos --recurse --template client.json --workers 4
71	```
72	
73	`client.json`:
74	
75	```json
76	{
77	  "name": "Acme Wedding Co",
78	  "byline": "Jane Doe Photography",
79	  "location": "Austin, TX",
80	  "keywordsPrefix": ["wedding", "acme-co"],
81	  "captionPrefix": "Acme Wedding Co:"
82	}
83	```
84	
85	### Build a catalog
86	
87	```
88	imagearc catalog ./photos --recurse -o catalog.csv
89	```
90	
91	`--no-ai` is the default and only mode today: catalog reads existing IPTC/XMP
92	via ExifTool and writes CSV — no network, no AI calls.
93	
94	### Serve the web UI
95	
96	```
97	imagearc serve --addr localhost:8733
98	```
99	
100	Opens your browser to a dark-themed UI for batch captioning (with a live
101	activity log), a metadata editor, and catalog building.
102	
103	### Version
104	
105	```
106	imagearc version
107	```
108	
109	## How metadata is written
110	
111	ImageArc maps fields to both IPTC and XMP so readers using either standard pick
112	them up:
113	
114	| Field    | IPTC             | XMP                  |
115	|----------|------------------|-----------------------|
116	| Caption  | Caption-Abstract | dc:Description        |
117	| Keywords | Keywords         | dc:Subject             |
118	| Byline   | By-line          | dc:Creator              |
119	| Location | Sub-location     | iptcCore:Location        |
120	| Headline | Headline         | —                          |
121	| Date     | DateCreated      | —                          |
122	
123	For RAW files, a `<file>.xmp` sidecar is created (if missing) and then updated
124	via ExifTool — the original RAW file is never touched. For JPEG/TIFF, ExifTool
125	writes in place with `-overwrite_original` (a backup is skipped since nothing
126	but metadata changes, and the pixel data is untouched by ExifTool tag writes).
127	
128	## Use with Shotwell / other photo managers
129	
130	ImageArc writes standard IPTC/XMP, so photo managers that read embedded
131	metadata (Shotwell, digiKam, Lightroom, darktable) pick up the AI captions and
132	keywords as tags — no plugin needed. A right-click **"Caption with ImageArc"**
133	action for GNOME Files/Nemo/Caja and the Shotwell workflow are in
134	[integrations/](integrations/README.md).
135	
136	## No lock-in
137	
138	- Catalogs are plain CSV.
139	- Templates are plain JSON.
140	- Metadata lives in your files/sidecars as standard IPTC/XMP, readable by
141	  Lightroom, digiKam, darktable, or any other tool.
142	
143	## Repositories
144	
145	Mirrored on both forges — clone from whichever you prefer:
146	
147	- Codeberg: https://codeberg.org/YR-Design/imagearc
148	- GitHub: https://github.com/robouden/imagearc
149	
150	## Credits
151	
152	Offline reverse geocoding (GPS → city/country) uses the
153	[GeoNames](https://www.geonames.org/) `cities15000` dataset, licensed
154	[CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).
155	
156	## License
157	
158	[CC0 1.0 Universal](LICENSE) — public domain dedication. No rights reserved.
159

input.new_string:
## Origin

The concept behind ImageArc lived in my mind for years, discussed at length with
fellow photographers like Paul Disario and Michael Goldberg. It was finally
woken up and set in motion by Peter Blakely's Mac-only FotoArch/MetaEdit —
ImageArc grew out of the wish for a free, open-source, cross-platform take on
that idea.

## Credits

result.output: The file /home/rob/Documents/Open source fotoapp/README.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: Added an **Origin** section before Credits. (The spell-checker flags are just proper nouns/product names.)
Can yuo add a lin to Peter his project?

input.query: Peter Blakeley FotoArch MetaEdit Mac photo metadata app

result.output:
Web search results for query: "Peter Blakeley FotoArch MetaEdit Mac photo metadata app"

Links: [{"title":"Photo Meta Edit 3.06 is available on the Mac App Store","url":"https://www.tranquillitybase.jp/PME/PhotoMetaEdit_en.html"},{"title":"Metalmage — Download Mac app on Setapp | Automated image data editing | Setapp","url":"https://setapp.com/apps/metaimage"},{"title":"MetaEdit","url":"https://www.metaedit.app/"},{"title":"Photo Meta Edit on the Mac App Store - Apple","url":"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/photo-meta-edit/id553760117?mt=12"},{"title":"Download Photo Meta Edit for Mac | MacUpdate","url":"https://www.macupdate.com/app/mac/45375"},{"title":"Modifying original metadata of photos inside photo library","url":"https://discussions.apple.com/thread/254853029"},{"title":"Here’s How to Edit Photo Metadata on Mac (Easy) | Medium","url":"https://appinitio.medium.com/editing-photo-metadata-on-mac-is-easier-than-you-think-acd65f10c265"},{"title":"Best Open Source Mac Metadata Editors 2026","url":"https://sourceforge.net/directory/metadata-editors/mac/"},{"title":"apps.apple.com","url":"https://apps.apple.com/ca/app/metaedit/id6450723279"},{"title":"MetaImage | NeededApps","url":"https://neededapps.com/metaimage/"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about photo metadata editing apps for Mac:

Photo Meta Edit is available by Tranquillity Base on the App Store, and it allows you to easily edit photo metadata, Exif, GPS, IPTC, XMP and other types, in JPG and Tiff files.

There's also MetaEdit, a tool for tagging photos with custom metadata, which can also be used to tag photos with custom keywords to be searched by the Apple Photos app.

However, the search results don't contain specific information about Peter Blakeley or FotoArch in connection with these apps. The search returned general results about Mac photo metadata editing applications, but didn't find details linking those specific names to these tools.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: I couldn't confirm Peter's exact project URL from search — a few candidates came up but none clearly tied to "Peter Blakeley / FotoArch". To avoid linking the wrong app, which is his?

questions.question: Which link should point to Peter's project?

options.label: MetaEdit (metaedit.app)
options.description: https://www.metaedit.app/ — a Mac keyword/metadata tagging app.

options.label: Photo Meta Edit
options.description: https://www.tranquillitybase.jp/PME/ — Mac App Store metadata editor by Tranquillity Base.

options.label: I'll paste the URL
options.description: You give me the exact link to Peter's FotoArch/MetaEdit.

result.output: The user answered: "Which link should point to Peter's project?"="https://fotoarch.app/#beta". Read the answers carefully — they may request clarification, changes, or that you not proceed — and follow what they actually say.
The file /home/rob/Documents/Open source fotoapp/README.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text: Linked to https://fotoarch.app/#beta. (The "Foto" flag is just the spell-checker on a product name.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push
